_comment_chapters: Each chapter is a moment in the story. Order is determined by position in this list. Images can be remote URLs or local paths like 'assets/photo.jpg'. Set image to null if there is none. Set video to a local path for video chapters — video takes priority over image.

chapters.title: Year 1 - Mapping a Beginning from a Distance
chapters.body: The journey of Cohort 2 at VinUniversity began in an unusual landscape: online classrooms through Zoom screens, where students met professors and classmates as small digital windows rather than offline campus. Although I did not struggle with my academic performance, I had a very hard time to imagine all of the connection, belonging, and community before I could actually meet everyone. Therefore, I felt so lost and could not have any close friends at the beginning.

chapters.title: Year 1 - From Strangers to Friends on VinUni Campus
chapters.body: When 300 cohort-2 students finally arrived on campus in the second semester, the dormitory became more than a residence, it became the first real meeting point on the VinUni journey map. During quarantine periods, shared routines and limited social exposure unexpectedly created space for deeper conversations and stronger bonds and I became part of a friend group called “lâu đài tình ái” [a loving castel]. This showed how community at VinUni often begins not through formal structures, but through everyday moments of living together.

chapters.title: Year 1 - Friendship During the Pandemic, an Identical Memory of Our Cohort
chapters.body: Outside academic learning hours, evenings in the dormitory became spaces where the sense of belongings truly took shape. Music, movies, late-night karaoke, and long conversations transformed a group of individuals into a supporting network. Whenever I needed help, there was always somebody willing to be there for me and eventually, this friend group became my first anchor at VinUni.

chapters.title: Year 2 - When the Path Forward Becomes Unclear
chapters.body: At VinUniversity, it is common to see students sharing milestones on social media: excellent transcripts, club president champion of change, dean’s list, president’s list. While these achievements help some students stay motivated, others like me begin to feel pressured because my future uncertainty is more visible. In the second year, I became more reflective and started questioning: how the knowledge from courses like Linear Algebra or Advanced Probability and Statistics connected to my future, and what exactly do I want to do as a career. These unsolved anxiety drive me to withdraw in academic performance with missing assignments and lower-grade in GPA.

chapters.title: Year 2 - Finding Support from People at VinUniversity
chapters.body: Second year is also the time when the student journey at VinUniversity often begins expanding beyond classrooms and dormitories into shared apartments and more independent routines. Living together off campus created new rhythms of daily life, such as cooking, singing at the amphitheater, and long conversations about uncertainty and possibility. As our friendship grew stronger, I became more open to talk about my mixed feelings and personal struggle in studying. While I clearly like the major, I could not make the learning journey as enjoyable as before anymore.

chapters.title: Year 2 - Pausing for Reflection and Connecting with The Self
chapters.body: Watching my friends studying hard at the 24/7 learning area while I could not, my mind drifted back to high school, when I spent sleepless nights solving programming problems and preparing for contests. Back then, the satisfaction of cracking a difficult problem felt far more meaningful, and earning a medal was just a milestone, not a destination. I start to feel the urge of having those days back, when I really enjoy what I was doing and I clearly know the purpose of doing each task. This thought drive me to a decision of taking a gap year to rediscover my interests and fortunately, my good friends was supportive enough to encourage me to execute this brave decision.

chapters.title: Gap Year - Go Home to Find Myself
Gap Year - A Necessary Gap to Move Forward with Clarity
chapters.body: Taking a gap year now felt more like an intentional turning point rather than a running away from the problem. Stepping away from the regular academic timeline, I was allowed to spend time at home, playing with kids and reading more of my favorite anime comics, which thereby helped me with staying connected to my true self. This fueled me to reach out for an opportunity that I have always been curious and dreamed of but too scared to do: joining a summer AI program and applying for an AI Engineer position and spending about five months working full-time, from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Interestingly, several students within the same living community at VinUni made similar decisions at different moments: my second-year roommate, also a VinUni student in CECS, took a gap year at the same time - without either of us knowing beforehand. In fact, all four of my roommates from that year eventually took gap years, just at different times. This shows that “uncommon” timelines are not that uncommon. These detours reflect how student journeys are shaped not by a single fixed route, but by the willingness to explore before continuing forward with clearer purpose.

chapters.title: Year 3 - Returning to a Familiar Place That Feels Different - VinUniversity Campus
chapters.body: Returning to VinUniversity after a gap year felt both familiar and strange at the same time: Study rooms that were once filled with close friends were now shared with younger cohorts, while many former classmates were already preparing for graduation and their next steps. My friendgroup gatherings became less frequent, and places like the dormitory and amphitheater felt quieter than before. Yet the friendships formed in earlier years did not disappear, we just simply changed the way we support each other. This stage of the journey reflects how university spaces evolve alongside students themselves, reminding us that belonging is not tied to a single moment, but continues through connections that adapt over time.

chapters.title: Year 3 - Rediscovering Passion Through Research
chapters.body: Returning to campus also brought a renewed connection to the academic learning process. Compared to the structured, repetitive rhythm of full-time industry work, the university environment offered space to think more freely and explore ideas more deeply alongside peers. I also began working on research with professors at VinUni and a partner university in the United States gradually reshaped academic motivation into a clearer path forward. Long days spent reading papers and coding in learning spaces such as the Mac Lab and 24/7 study areas became part of a new routine. Through these experiences, research emerged not only as an academic activity, but as a direction that allowed freedom to think, to explore my passion, to fulfill my curiosity and even to work on my own schedule.

chapters.title: Year 4 - Meeting Mentors Who Expand the Map Forward
chapters.body: During the final year at VinUniversity, participating in an exchange semester at Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST) became an important extension of the academic journey beyond campus borders. This is where I honorably got to work with Professor May Fung who offered a glimpse of what supportive mentorship in research can look like: guidance that makes complex ideas feel approachable and future paths feel possible. Through collaboration with both academic and industry partners, our research project developed during this exchange is now being prepared for patent submission. Getting connected with a dedicated mentor can help students stay motivated to continue pursuing their goals, especially when they can see people already working in the field and flourishing in the present. Such mentorship makes future pathways feel more visible and worth trying hard for during undergraduate years.

chapters.title: Year 4 - Familiar Feelings in a New Place
chapters.body: Meeting Vietnamese freshmen at HKUST often brought back memories of the early days at VinUniversity, especially the friendships first formed during dormitory life. At the same time, daily walks between the dorm and library gradually became part of a new routine, creating a sense of familiarity in an unfamiliar environment and helping shape the rhythm of life during the exchange semester. Yet whenever I accidentally came across photos of VinUniversity on social media, I found myself missing the campus more than I had anticipated. It was during these moments that I realized how much my relationship with the university had changed: from once feeling uncertain about where I belonged to gradually seeing VinUniversity as a place that felt like home and an important part of my sense of belonging.

chapters.title: Year 4 - Exploring Myself beyond My Major
chapters.body: Returning to VinUniversity in the final semester created space for me to explore courses beyond the CECS major, including Digital Arts and Sciences and Arts Appreciation. Through activities like drawing, sketching, photography and small digital humanities projects, I discovered new creative interests I had never explored before, and I unexpectedly found them both enjoyable and meaningful. Besides that, interacting with first- and second-year students also brought quiet reminders of earlier stages of the journey. Experiences like these show how the final year is not only a time for completion, but also a moment to reconnect with curiosity and continue exploring interests that may shape future directions in unexpected ways.

chapters.title: Year 4 - Holding Onto the Final Moments
chapters.body: As my graduation approaching in June 2026, everyday spaces on VinUni campus began to feel different for me: not because they changed, but because their meaning became clearer in time. Walking through familiar paths created a quiet awareness that this stage of the journey was coming to a close. It feels almost unreal how much I have changed from a shy boy, clueless with what I’m doing to a more confident boy, happy with the present and knowing where I am going in the future. Moments like these often bring reflections not only on personal experiences, but also on friends who had already moved forward into their own next chapters. Sometimes I wonder if my friends who graduated last year would feel the same way. The final year becomes a space for noticing what might once have felt ordinary, allowing students to carry these memories with them as they prepare to continue their journeys beyond campus.
